general.name: General Narrative Arc
general.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: Standard 3-Act Short-Form Narrative
Structure:
1. Hook (0-3s): Capture immediate attention.
2. Body (3-45s): Deliver core value, insight, or story related to {{goal}}.
3. Climax & CTA (45-60s): Deliver strong payoff and direct action prompt for {{audience}}.
</copywriting_framework>

pas.name: PAS (Problem - Agitation - Solution)
pas.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: PAS (Problem - Agitation - Solution)
Structure:
1. Problem (0-5s): State a sharp, painful problem facing {{audience}} in {{industry}}.
2. Agitation (5-35s): Deepen the emotional and practical cost of ignoring this problem. Why do standard fixes fail?
3. Solution (35-60s): Present the breakthrough solution that resolves the pain and achieves {{goal}}, transitioning to the CTA.
</copywriting_framework>

hormozi.name: Hormozi Value Equation
hormozi.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: Alex Hormozi Value Equation & Grand Slam Offer
Structure:
1. Dream Outcome (0-10s): Paint a vivid picture of achieving {{goal}} without usual trade-offs.
2. Perceived Likelihood of Achievement (10-25s): Provide undeniable proof, case studies, or logical certainty for {{audience}}.
3. Time Delay & Effort Reduction (25-45s): Show how this eliminates waiting, friction, and hard labor in {{industry}}.
4. No-Brainer CTA (45-60s): Make an irresistible offer that would be foolish to ignore.
</copywriting_framework>

storybrand.name: StoryBrand Narrative
storybrand.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: Donald Miller StoryBrand Narrative Arc
Structure:
1. The Hero & The Problem (0-10s): Position {{audience}} as the hero facing an external and internal struggle in {{industry}}.
2. The Guide (10-25s): Introduce {{brand}} as the empathetic, authoritative guide who understands their struggle.
3. The Plan (25-40s): Give the hero a simple 3-step roadmap to overcome the obstacle and achieve {{goal}}.
4. Call to Action & Stakes (40-60s): Call them to action, painting the success they will enjoy and the failure they will avoid.
</copywriting_framework>

aida.name: AIDA (Attention - Interest - Desire - Action)
aida.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: AIDA (Attention - Interest - Desire - Action)
Structure:
1. Attention (0-3s): Hook {{audience}} with an arresting statement or visual pattern interrupt.
2. Interest (3-20s): Engage intellectual curiosity with unusual facts, industry shifts in {{industry}}, or counterintuitive insights.
3. Desire (20-45s): Trigger emotional yearning by showing the transformation and benefits of achieving {{goal}}.
4. Action (45-60s): Deliver a clear, urgent directive telling them exactly what step to take next.
</copywriting_framework>

bab.name: BAB (Before - After - Bridge)
bab.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: BAB (Before - After - Bridge)
Structure:
1. Before (0-15s): Describe the current frustrating reality of {{audience}} struggling in {{industry}}.
2. After (15-35s): Describe a world where the struggle is gone and {{goal}} has been completely achieved.
3. Bridge (35-60s): Reveal the exact mechanism, product, or strategy that bridges the gap from Before to After, closing with a CTA.
</copywriting_framework>

quest.name: QUEST Copywriting Formula
quest.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: QUEST (Qualify - Understand - Educate - Stimulate - Transition)
Structure:
1. Qualify (0-5s): Call out the exact target profile: 'If you are a {{audience}} in {{industry}}...'
2. Understand (5-15s): Show deep empathy for their daily challenges and aspirations towards {{goal}}.
3. Educate (15-35s): Teach a valuable, actionable concept or framework that changes their perspective.
4. Stimulate (35-50s): Build excitement and urgency around implementing this insight immediately.
5. Transition (50-60s): Seamlessly transition to the call to action.
</copywriting_framework>

fab.name: FAB (Features - Advantages - Benefits)
fab.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: FAB (Features - Advantages - Benefits)
Structure:
1. Features (0-15s): Introduce the tangible characteristics of the solution or method in {{industry}}.
2. Advantages (15-35s): Explain why these features are superior to conventional alternatives for {{audience}}.
3. Benefits (35-60s): Translate advantages into emotional, real-world payoffs (saving time, making money, achieving {{goal}}), leading into the CTA.
</copywriting_framework>

4u.name: 4U (Urgent - Useful - Ultra-specific - Unique)
4u.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: 4U (Urgent - Useful - Ultra-specific - Unique)
Structure:
1. Urgent (0-10s): Why {{audience}} must pay attention right now in 2026.
2. Useful (10-25s): How this immediately solves a pressing bottleneck in {{industry}}.
3. Ultra-specific (25-45s): Provide exact numbers, steps, or data points to achieve {{goal}}.
4. Unique (45-60s): Highlight the proprietary differentiator of {{brand}}, closing with an urgent CTA.
</copywriting_framework>

acca.name: ACCA (Awareness - Comprehension - Conviction - Action)
acca.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: ACCA (Awareness - Comprehension - Conviction - Action)
Structure:
1. Awareness (0-10s): Make {{audience}} aware of a critical trend or hidden problem in {{industry}}.
2. Comprehension (10-30s): Explain clearly why this is happening and how it impacts their journey towards {{goal}}.
3. Conviction (30-45s): Present undeniable evidence, logic, or social proof to create absolute belief in the solution.
4. Action (45-60s): Give a direct, unmistakable command on how to take the next step.
</copywriting_framework>

russell_brunson.name: Russell Brunson Epiphany Bridge
russell_brunson.template:
<copywriting_framework>
Framework: Russell Brunson Epiphany Bridge Arc
Structure:
1. Backstory & The Wall (0-15s): Share the relatable struggle of hitting a brick wall trying to achieve {{goal}} using traditional {{industry}} advice.
2. The Epiphany (15-35s): Describe the aha-moment discovery that destroyed the old belief and revealed the new opportunity.
3. The New Opportunity (35-50s): Show how this new vehicle makes achieving {{goal}} faster, easier, and more certain for {{audience}}.
4. The Offer & CTA (50-60s): Invite them to step into the new opportunity today.
</copywriting_framework>
